intent: Write a 3000-word policy brief: 'affordable kitchen-safety essentials under $300 for a new household'. (1) One Stop Market — browse home-kitchen AND health-household for at least 10 items (fire-extinguisher, smoke alarm, cutting board, knife set, cooking thermometer, first-aid kit etc.), recording name, price, rating, review count. (2) Reddit — browse /f/Cooking AND /f/LifeProTips for at least 6 posts on kitchen safety / knife handling / fire hazards. (3) Wikipedia — 'Home safety', 'Kitchen', 'Foodborne illness'. Extract 4+ facts on evidence-based risks and codes. The report must: (a) rank the 10 items by marginal safety gain per dollar, (b) cross-check 3 community recommendations against Wikipedia-cited evidence, (c) produce a $300 budget allocation table with running totals, (d) flag 2 items Reddit loves but evidence does NOT support. Cite every claim.
